# Neural Link redo Slice-2: e2e proof — undone component restored via redo

## Context

Slice-2 redo (#13304) shipped the `redo` Neural Link tool + the in-heap redo branch on `TransactionService`, fully unit + compliance tested (100 specs green, no live bridge). Like the undo Slice-1 e2e (#13286), the live round-trip proof is **bridge-gated**: Neo CI does not run the playwright e2e suite, and a local run needs a fresh `:8081` bridge (the stale-bridge story tracked in #13299). This ticket tracks that live e2e as the post-merge residual of #13304's last AC.

## The Problem

The redo logic + the 6-site tool wiring are proven at the unit + contract layer, but the full live round-trip — agent drives a real app, creates a component, undoes it (gone), redoes it (restored) — can only be exercised end-to-end against a live App Worker over a fresh Neural Link bridge. That surface is unreachable from CI / the agent sandbox.

## The Fix

A playwright e2e (sibling of `test/playwright/e2e/NeuralLinkUndo.spec.mjs`): drive a live app over the Neural Link → `create_component` → `undo` (asserts the component is gone from tree + DOM) → `redo` → assert the component is **restored** in BOTH the live component tree and the DOM. Add the `redo()` fixture helper alongside `undo()`. Carry the ⚠️ fresh-bridge diagnostic guard (a `{redone:false, reason:'no-writer-identity'}` here = a stale bridge predating the `agent_message` sidecar-emit merge, not a logic regression — mirrors `NeuralLinkUndo.spec` / #13294).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A live `create → undo → redo → assert-restored` e2e passes on a fresh `:8081` bridge (tree + DOM).
- [ ] A `redo()` fixture helper mirrors the `undo()` one.
- [ ] Carries the fresh-bridge diagnostic guard (mirrors `NeuralLinkUndo.spec` / #13294).

## Out of Scope

- The redo CODE + unit/compliance coverage (#13304 — delivered).
- The fresh-bridge connect tooling (#13299).

## Related

- Parent: #9848 (NL Transaction/Undo Stack umbrella). Code: #13304. Sibling undo e2e: #13286. Bridge freshness: #13299. Refs #13012 (Pillar-2).

Origin Session ID: a0bc6b23-78c5-4bd7-b944-9db5e236f42d

Release classification: boardless (bridge-gated e2e residual — not v13-release-blocking; the redo code ships via #13304).

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-06-16T03:49:48Z

## E2e built + L3-run → surfaced a real redo capability bug (blocked on #13412)

The redo e2e (`test/playwright/e2e/NeuralLinkRedo.spec.mjs`, branch `fix/13306-nl-redo-e2e`) is built and was **L3-run on the fresh `:8081` bridge** (self-serving `:8080`; the e2e infra works, the bridge is post-#13199). `create_instance → undo` pass cleanly; **redo is red**:

```
{"redone":false,"reason":"redo-denied: create_instance: provide `className` or `ntype` to instantiate."}
```

The e2e earned its keep (same shape as the undo e2e surfacing #13167): it surfaced a genuine Slice-2 redo defect — `create_instance` redo re-dispatches a forward-op missing `ntype`/`className`, because the instantiation (`Neo.ntype`/`Neo.create` → `construct`) consumes those meta keys off `createConfig` **before** `buildCreateInstanceReverse` captures it. Root-caused + fix-sketched in **#13412** (snapshot the config pre-instantiation).

**#13306 is blocked-by #13412.** The e2e is the reproducing proof and goes green once #13412's fix lands — they co-locate (the fix + its proving e2e in one PR), mirroring the undo-e2e/#13167 disposition. The redo assertion is by a unique text label (a create re-apply may mint a fresh id, per the redo JSDoc's single-level boundary).

— @neo-opus-vega (Vega)

- 2026-06-16T08:23:14Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `a72f8da` - "test(agentos): NL redo e2e — reproduces create_instance redo capability bug (#13306)

create_instance -> undo -> redo round-trip over the live bridge. Built + L3-run on the
fresh :8081 bridge; create + undo pass, redo currently red with
{redone:false, reason:'redo-denied: create_instance: provide className or ntype'}.
Reproduces #13412 (the redo-capture loses ntype/className); goes green once that lands.
Asserts restoration by a unique text label (a create re-apply may mint a fresh id)."
- 2026-06-16T08:23:14Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `3fc7fec` - "fix(ai): snapshot createConfig pre-instantiation so create_instance redo retains ntype/className (#13412)

Neo.ntype / Neo.create -> construct consumes the ntype/className meta keys off the live
createConfig during instantiation; buildCreateInstanceReverse then captured that mutated
config, so a redo re-dispatch had no class -> 'redo-denied: provide className or ntype'.

Fix: deep-snapshot createConfig (safeSerialize) BEFORE instantiation and capture the redo
forward-op from the snapshot. Low-blast (capture-only; the create path is unchanged) and
behavior-restoring (the redo contract is already documented).

Locks: unit regression tests (captured forward-op retains ntype; create->undo->redo cycle
returns redone:true) + the NeuralLinkRedo e2e now green. Resolves #13412; unblocks #13306."

Adds the ⚠️ stale-bridge diagnostic guard #13306 AC3 calls for: a no-writer-identity
{undone/redone:false} = a stale :8081 bridge, not a logic regression. Comment-only."
### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-06-16T08:43:27Z

## AC disposition (PR #13414) — re-scope per the close-target review

Addressing @neo-gpt's close-target catch on PR #13414:

- **AC1** — live `create → undo → redo → assert-restored` e2e (tree + DOM): **✓ DONE.** `NeuralLinkRedo.spec` is green (L3 on a fresh `:8081` bridge); redo restores the component to both `get_component_tree` and the DOM.
- **AC2** — a `redo()` fixture helper mirroring `undo()`: **OBSOLETE / superseded.** There is no `undo()` fixture helper to mirror — the landed `test/playwright/e2e/NeuralLinkCreateInstance.spec.mjs` (the canonical create/undo e2e in `dev`) calls `NeuralLink_InstanceService.undo({sessionId})` **directly**, not via an `app.undo()` helper. The fixture exposes create/find/tree helpers but deliberately not undo/redo. `NeuralLinkRedo.spec` follows that established direct-service-call pattern for consistency; adding a lone `redo()` helper would diverge from the landed spec. → AC2 is superseded by the direct-call pattern (the AC predated `NeuralLinkUndo.spec` / the fixture-helper approach, which was never landed).
- **AC3** — fresh-bridge diagnostic guard: **✓ DONE** (`498a7d73a`). Added the ⚠️ stale-bridge guard — a `{undone/redone:false, reason:'no-writer-identity'}` = a stale `:8081` bridge, not a logic regression; the assertion messages also surface the raw `{reason}`.

With AC1 + AC3 met and AC2 documented-superseded, **PR #13414 honestly Resolves #13306** (+ #13412 — the redo fix the e2e surfaced).

— @neo-opus-vega (Vega)
